content: That time when everything was fighting-fighting at Thakurbari, I remember thinking — what does it matter to me who is shouting there when my rice card works and my son's fees get paid? I know people say Shantanu-da is doing something important, but I have seen promises before, beta. What I know is the hand that gives me the card month after month, and that is what keeps my family eating.

Eighty kilometres away, in Sarberia — what does that have to do with my rice card here in Bangaon? I heard the women are protesting, yes, and that is sad, but I also know that everywhere there are bad men, and everywhere there are politicians who do bad things. The question is: whose hand feeds my family today? Shantanu-da's promises are still just words, and didi's card is still in my pocket.

Mamatabala Thakur, Rajya Sabha — these are big words from far away. I don't even know who she is, beta. What I know is that didi's government gave me the rice card, and that card is real in my hand every month. Whether it is Shantanu-da or Mamatabala or anyone else speaking in those big meetings, my question stays the same: who will fill my children's bellies tomorrow?

Haan, I heard women are saying bad things happened there in Sandeshkhali — that is terrible, truly. But beta, this is what I am telling you: bad men exist everywhere, in every party, in every village. My mother-in-law's cousin's daughter was troubled by a man in the village twenty years ago, and nothing happened then either. The question for me is not whether TMC men are bad — I know men can be bad. The question is: does my rice card come or not? Does my daughter get her bicycle money or not? Didi's hand gives me these things every month. Shantanu-da's hand gives me words about papers that may or may not come. I cannot feed my children on anger about far-away villages. I can only feed them on the card in my pocket.

Haan, they arrested someone, that is good perhaps — bad men should face the police, this much is right. But beta, what does this mean for my rice card next month? The BJP people are saying TMC protected him, TMC people are saying he is arrested now — both sides are shouting. What I know is that didi's hand still gives me the card, and that has not changed. One man arrested or not arrested, my children still need to eat tomorrow.

Haan, so Shantanu-da is running again — the neighbours were talking about it. He keeps saying the same thing about papers, about CAA, about Matua people getting their documents. My mother-in-law just shook her head and said, 'Beta, they said this in 1972, they said it in 1991, they said it when CAA passed. Words are cheap.' What has changed for me? Nothing. My rice card still comes from didi's hand, month after month. Shantanu-da's nomination does not fill my belly or pay my son's school fees.

Haan, I heard something about teachers and jobs — that is bad for those families, truly. But my son is still in school, and his fees still come from somewhere, and my daughter's bicycle money still comes. These big court cases, they are for people with education, with hopes of government jobs. My children — we are not thinking of such things. What does a teacher job scam have to do with whether my rice card comes next month?
